what_it_does: Tracks the deep-rebuild loop completion criteria, their required verification protocol, ordered execution sequence, dated checkoffs, by-use evidence, form checks, deviations, gaps, and closing gate status.
observations: Defines a verification protocol that requires one criterion at a time, six proofs green, server smoke, function and form checks, dated marking, and a scoped commit.
Declares a fixed priority order from E1 through P-close and names the first unchecked item as the source for what comes next.
All listed criteria E1, E2, E3, M1-M5, C1-C3, and P1 are marked complete on 2026-06-13.
Each criterion records FUNCTION and FORM faces, with evidence framed as browser use, route checks, lint/proof results, screenshots, or live edits.
Several entries include honest deviations, gaps, notes, and captured backlog items rather than treating the checklist as immutable.
standouts: The file treats completion as requiring both behavioral proof and visual/form verification.
The monolith decomposition is recorded as a staged sequence from gallery infrastructure through a thin index shell.
The content loop is recorded as a three-part arc: content home, address-resolving renderer, and grown content output.
The closing P1 gate summarizes route health, room rendering, screenshots, and known non-blocking count drift.
conventions: Uses section prefixes E, M, C, and P to group criteria by concern.
Uses paired FUNCTION and FORM paragraphs for each criterion.
Uses dated checkbox markers and explicit by-use or verified labels.
Uses uppercase labels for protocol, priority, deviations, gaps, scope, notes, infra, captured, and known items.
Keeps evidence inline with the criterion rather than in a separate table.
concerns: The file is dense and mixes checklist state, evidence log, deviations, backlog captures, and readiness narrative in one document.
Several criteria contain long paragraphs with many facts, which makes individual evidence items hard to address independently.
Some claims depend on referenced commands, screenshots, routes, and other files that are not present inside this file.
notes: Brief this as the closed ledger for a 2026-06-13 deep-rebuild loop, with each criterion carrying both function evidence and form evidence.
questions: Where are the screenshots and proof outputs referenced by the criteria stored?
Which backlog entries correspond to the H1 and H3 captures named here?
What is the expected process after consulting docs/BACKLOG.md HORIZON 1+?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents a truth-table-style completion ledger and records verification results for each criterion.
apparent_themes: Function and form as paired completion faces
Honest capture of deviations and gaps
intention_signals: The file seems intended to keep a rebuild effort disciplined by making each step complete only when runtime behavior, route health, visual form, and evidence are all recorded.
novelty: It combines a completion checklist with dated verification evidence, visual acceptance notes, architectural narrative, and backlog capture in the same file.
contribution: Contributes a process ledger for completion gating: ordered criteria, dual function/form acceptance, by-use verification habits, dated status, and recorded exceptions that a fused system would preserve as operational memory.
summary_for_embedding: A completed 2026-06-13 deep-rebuild criteria ledger for counterpart-design documenting ordered E/M/C/P tasks, verification protocol, by-use evidence, form checks, deviations, and final estate gate status.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for controlling and recording a rebuild loop so each criterion was marked complete only after proof runs, live behavior checks, visual/form verification, and commit discipline.
